generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the swinging motion of the hoist to provide real-time angular displacement data for payload stability analysis and trajectory correction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors wind speed in the lab environment to account for external disturbances affecting crane operations and payload dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData WindSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneStateLogger : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuously logs the state of the physical crane (joint angles, motor currents, payload position) via the Physical Crane Interface and transmits to TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance SwingAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance WindSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance CraneStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneStateLogger
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Represents the geometric and kinematic constraints of the gantry crane, including joint definitions, payload dynamics, and trajectory paths. Used for trajectory planning and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonControlEnabler, SimulationServiceEnabler
]

instance CADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D representation of the gantry crane's mechanical structure and container pucks, generated using Autodesk Fusion 360. Used for real-time visualization and animation synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationServiceEnabler, SimulationServiceEnabler
]

instance TimeseriesCraneData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured time-series data of crane operational metrics (joint angles, motor currents, payload position, timestamps) stored in TimescaleDB for historical analysis and model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateDataTransmitted, SwingAngleDataTransmitted, WindSpeedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler, HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance SimulationOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted crane behavior and trajectory results generated by the Simulation Service based on control inputs and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationServiceEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PythonControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based control application (gantrylib) implementing real-time trajectory generation, optimal control logic, and kinematic model execution."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high-write/read workloads, storing historical and real-time crane state data for querying, analysis, and model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto-based MQTT broker enabling low-latency, bidirectional communication between physical crane components and digital twin services."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalCraneInterfaceEnabler, OptimalControlService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance DockerContainerizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Docker-based containerization platform for packaging and deploying modular digital twin services (e.g., Logging Application, Dashboard) ensuring reproducibility and scalability."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalCraneInterfaceEnabler, VisualizationServiceEnabler, SimulationServiceEnabler
]

instance SimulationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service executing trajectory simulations based on predefined control inputs and environmental conditions, dynamically interacting with CAD and kinematic models."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance VisualizationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables real-time and historical visualization of crane state via interactive dashboards or 3D CAD renderings (e.g., ROS RViz or Unity)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance PhysicalCraneInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python program acting as intermediary between physical crane controllers (Arduino, TMC4671-Eval) and digital twin services, handling bidirectional data flow via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService, HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance GitHubRepositoryEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source GitHub repository hosted by Cosys-Lab containing code, models, and setup instructions for reproducible research and education."
    DTDFVocab:enables DocumentationService
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements model-based control algorithms (PID, MPC) to optimize crane motion trajectories, payload stability, and energy efficiency using precomputed control inputs from the kinematic model."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight, DisturbanceRejectionAction, ConstraintEnforcementAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously records time-series data from the physical crane and stores it in TimescaleDB for post-mortem analysis, combining sensor data with simulation outputs for cross-validation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataFusionInsight, QueryableArchiveInsight, AnomalyTaggingInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time and historical visualization of crane state via interactive dashboards or 3D CAD renderings, syncing animations with physical movements (e.g., ROS RViz)."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveTelemetryInsight, AnimationSyncInsight, CustomizableViewsInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements real-time synchronization and discrepancy detection between physical and digital twins using state estimation (Kalman filters), residual analysis, and adaptive correction."
    DTDFVocab:provides StateEstimationInsight, DiscrepancyDetectionInsight, AdaptiveCorrectionAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitors crane for abnormal behaviors (motor stalls, cable slack) using machine learning models (SVM, Isolation Forests) and rule-based systems for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultIsolationInsight, SeverityScoringInsight, AutomatedAlertAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes trajectory simulations based on control inputs and environmental conditions, validating and predicting system behavior in conjunction with CAD and kinematic models."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationOutputInsight, PredictionValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Collision-free, time-optimal paths for crane payload generated by the kinematic model and control algorithms."
]

instance DisturbanceRejectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Mitigates external disturbances (wind, payload shifts) via closed-loop feedback from the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ConstraintEnforcementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enforces hardware limits (joint velocity, torque) to prevent physical damage during crane operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataFusionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Combined sensor data and simulation outputs for cross-validation of physical and digital crane states."
]

instance QueryableArchiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "SQL-like querying capabilities for historical crane data (filtered by time, payload mass, control mode)."
]

instance AnomalyTaggingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Flags outliers or deviations (e.g., sudden velocity spikes) for fault diagnosis in crane operations."
]

instance LiveTelemetryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Streaming display of crane position, payload state, and operational metrics on web-based or desktop interfaces."
]

instance AnimationSyncInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization of CAD model animations with physical crane movements (e.g., using ROS RViz)."
]

instance CustomizableViewsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "User-defined metrics for domain-specific analysis (e.g., energy consumption, control error visualization)."
]

instance StateEstimationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Fused sensor data and kinematic model estimates of the physical crane's state (e.g., position, velocity)."
]

instance DiscrepancyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed discrepancy metrics (position error, velocity drift) to detect model-plant mismatches or sensor faults."
]

instance AdaptiveCorrectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts digital twin parameters (model coefficients) to minimize validation errors."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultIsolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of faulty actuators/sensors (e.g., jammed joint, broken encoder) in the crane system."
]

instance SeverityScoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Risk level assignment (low/medium/high) for detected faults based on impact analysis."
]

instance AutomatedAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers warnings when validation thresholds (e.g., RMSE  5%) are exceeded during crane operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SimulationOutputInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted crane behavior and trajectory results from simulation service for validation against physical operations."
]

instance PredictionValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison of simulation outputs with real-time physical crane data to validate model accuracy."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance CartMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls lateral movement of the crane cart via precise motor actuation, interfacing with the Arduino-based microcontroller."
]

instance HoistMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages vertical hoisting mechanism with electromagnet integration for container puck pickup/release, controlled via TMC4671-Eval motor driver."
]

]

// C1: Environment
instance LabScaleGantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Controlled lab environment for the lab-scale gantry crane system, including physical crane, sensors, actuators, and digital twin infrastructure."
    base:contains PhysicalCraneComponent, DigitalTwinComponent
]

instance PhysicalCraneComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gantry crane with kinematic constraints (joint angles, payload dynamics) representative of industrial systems but simplified for lab-scale experimentation."
]

instance DigitalTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital twin architecture deployed on a single computer with six containerized services (Logging Application, Dashboard, etc.) for real-time synchronization and analysis."
]

instance DocumentationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides technical documentation, tutorials, and setup instructions for the digital twin system."

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service** generate and transmit optimized movement trajectories to the **Physical Crane Interface**, which translates these into control commands for the physical crane. The **Trajectory Generator Service** leverages a kinematics model to produce trajectories designed to eliminate residual swinging motion at the end of movement. The **Continuous Validation Service** monitors executed trajectories by comparing real-time measurements with simulated trajectories, calculating distance metrics to assess deviations. If discrepancies exceed an unspecified threshold, operators are notified via the **Dashboard** for corrective action. No explicit details are provided on command types, message formats, validation/authentication mechanisms, feedback systems, or conflict resolution protocols beyond the distance metric comparison and operator notification. Command execution confirmation is implied through the validation process but not explicitly described."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents follow an **ad-hoc, component-based orchestration** rather than a standardized framework. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar uses a **hybrid architecture** combining:
  - **InfluxDB** (time-series database) for structured data storage,
  - **ActiveMQ** (message broker) for **event-driven communication** between physical sensors and the digital twin,
  - **Virtual machines (VMs) with prerecorded data** for offline simulation and behavioral adaptation.
  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs a **Microsoft Azure-based pipeline** with **AutomationML** for model-driven digital twin engineering, integrating:
  - **Raspberry Pi sensors** for real-time data acquisition,
  - **Simulation, prediction, and runtime visualization services** (likely deployed as microservices or containers),
  - **Azure tools** for orchestration (e.g., cloud-based workflow management).
  The **gantry crane system** (logistics/cyber-physical domain) is described using **C4 Model architecture**, implying a **containerized or modular decomposition** of components (e.g., crane control, container tracking, and coordination services). **Resource management** is not explicitly detailed, but the reliance on **virtualization/containerization** (e.g., VMs, Raspberry Pi) suggests **lightweight, scalable deployment**. **System-wide coordination** appears to be **use-case-specific**, with no mention of a unified framework; instead, **message brokers (ActiveMQ) and cloud platforms (Azure)** serve as integration backbones. The exemplars collectively demonstrate a **loosely coupled, domain-agnostic constellation** prioritizing **flexibility over standardization**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **Continuous Validation Service** that performs real-time comparisons between executed trajectories (derived from sensor data) and simulated trajectories (generated by the Simulation Service). This service quantifies discrepancies to identify sources of uncertainty, such as sensor noise, environmental disturbances, or model inaccuracies. Error detection mechanisms include threshold-based alerts for deviations exceeding predefined tolerances, triggering corrective actions such as recalibration of the Simulation Service or adjustments to control parameters. Quality assurance processes involve periodic validation of CAD models against physical measurements and iterative refinement of the Simulation Service to minimize prediction errors, ensuring high fidelity in the Digital Twin's representation of the gantry crane's behavior."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **MQTT Broker** facilitates signaling between services within the Digital Twin system, enabling asynchronous communication for trajectory generation, simulation, and validation processes. The **Physical Crane Interface** is implemented as a Python program connecting to the crane controllers, though no specific communication protocols (e.g., Modbus, OPC UA) or network topology details are provided. The **TimescaleDB database** serves as the central data repository for all system-generated data, with no mention of bandwidth, latency, or redundancy mechanisms. Security protocols for network communication are not specified, nor are details on network architecture (e.g., LAN, WAN) or reliability assurances beyond the implicit need for continuous operation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **on-premise** (co-located with the **Physical Crane**), with the following technical details:
  - **Hosting Platform**: Local servers or high-performance workstations running Python services (**Control Application**, **Logging Application**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, etc.). The **Database** (**TimescaleDB**) is deployed as a dedicated instance for time-series data.
  - **Computational Requirements**:
    - **Trajectory Generation**: Moderate CPU/GPU resources for real-time kinematic calculations (e.g., solving inverse kinematics for optimal paths).
    - **Simulation Service**: Requires sufficient RAM/CPU to model crane dynamics (e.g., rigid-body physics simulations).
    - **Dashboard**: Lightweight (Grafana) with minimal overhead.
  - **Storage Requirements**: **TimescaleDB** scales with historical data needs; no explicit limits are stated, but time-series compression is likely applied.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Vertical scaling (e.g., upgrading server hardware) is implied for the **Database** and computationally intensive services. Horizontal scaling (e.g., MQTT broker clustering) is not mentioned.
  - **Deployment Models**: Monolithic Python services with no explicit containerization (though the C4 model uses 'containers' as abstractions, not Docker). Services are likely deployed as standalone processes or virtual machines.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Achieved via:
    - **Continuous Validation Service** (proactive monitoring of trajectory deviations).
    - **Dashboard alerts** for operator intervention.
    - **TimescaleDB’s built-in redundancy** (if configured). No explicit HA clustering or backup strategies are described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems exhibit **limited but targeted horizontal integration** with external systems, primarily through **domain-specific protocols and cloud/edge technologies**. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar integrates with:
  - **InfluxDB** (external time-series database) for structured data persistence,
  - **ActiveMQ** (external message broker) for **pub/sub-based communication** between physical and digital components.
  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar demonstrates integration with:
  - **Microsoft Azure tools** (e.g., cloud services for simulation, prediction, and visualization),
  - **AutomationML** (industry-standard for **model-driven engineering and interoperability** in construction/automation domains),
  - **Raspberry Pi sensors** (external IoT devices for real-time data ingestion).
  Both exemplars rely on **ad-hoc integration patterns** rather than standardized Digital Twin frameworks, with **no explicit mention of enterprise system integration (e.g., ERP, MES)** or **cross-Digital Twin coordination protocols**. The **gantry crane system** (logistics domain) is not detailed enough to infer integration specifics, but its alignment with **C4 Model architecture** suggests potential for **modular integration** with **cyber-physical system (CPS) components** (e.g., container tracking systems, warehouse management software). **Interoperability standards** are only explicitly mentioned for **AutomationML** in the Air Quality Management exemplar, while other systems use **proprietary or lightweight protocols** (e.g., ActiveMQ, Azure APIs). Dependencies on external systems are **minimal and use-case-driven**, with no evidence of **federated Digital Twin architectures** or **standardized data exchange formats** beyond AutomationML."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system adheres to **AutomationML** for Digital Twin modeling (as used in the **Air Quality Management exemplar**) and employs the **C4 Model** for architectural documentation. Interoperability is ensured via an **MQTT Broker** (specifically **Eclipse Mosquitto**) for signaling between components, while **Microsoft Azure tools** are used in the Air Quality exemplar for pipeline implementation.
